title: Mike Vrabel to seek counseling, will reportedly skip Day 3 of NFL Draft

title: 2026 Red Sox are showing few signs of hope behind a dreadful offense

title: Haverhill police officer’s cause of death confirmed to be related to heart irregularity

title: Struggling Red Sox fall again to Yankees at Fenway 4-1
author: The Associated Press
bullets: Boston Red Sox Boston has scored two runs or fewer in six of its last eight games, losing five.
Red Sox pitcher Ranger Suarez heads to the dugout after getting pulled while trailing the New York Yankees in the fifth inning.

AP Photo/Charles Krupa BOSTON (AP) — Max Fried allowed three hits over eight scoreless innings, and the New York Yankees extended their winning streak to a season-high five with a 4-0 win over the Boston Red Sox on Wednesday night.

title: Sandwich man who ran ‘snack shack’ facing new charges of stealing from Plymouth schools
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Crime Patrick Van Cott is accused of stealing two refrigerators, a freezer, two hot plates, a griddle, a chargrill, a deep fryer, shelving, a sandwich prep table, a convection oven, and hanging chalk boards, among other supplies.
ShareSend this article to your social connections.

A man who allegedly stole thousands of dollars’ worth of food and equipment from Massachusetts public schools to fuel his “Snack Shack” is now facing federal fraud and theft charges, prosecutors said.
